body:
## Bug Description
When using a local model (e.g., DeepSeek Coder V2), the memory_search tool still tries to use OpenAI as the provider, resulting in 'No API key found for provider openai' error.

## Expected Behavior
Memory search should use the configured local model and not require an external API key.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Configure OpenClaw to use a local model (e.g., local/deepseek-coder-v2)
2. Attempt to use memory_search tool
3. Observe error: 'No API key found for provider openai'

## Environment
- OpenClaw version: 2026.1.30
- Model: local/deepseek-coder-v2
- Node: Christopher's Mac mini

## Suggested Fix
Configure memory_search to respect the local model setting rather than defaulting to OpenAI provider.

comments.body:
For me it actually indicates it was looking not only for OpenAI, but also Google, and Voyage:

```{
  "results": [],
  "disabled": true,
  "error": "No API key found for provider \"openai\". Auth store: /home/[userid]/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json (agentDir: /home/[userid]/.openclaw/agents/main/agent). Configure auth for this agent (openclaw agents add <id>) or copy auth-profiles.json from the main agentDir.\n\nNo API key found for provider \"google\". Auth store: /home/[userid]/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json (agentDir: /home/[userid]/.openclaw/agents/main/agent). Configure auth for this agent (openclaw agents add <id>) or copy auth-profiles.json from the main agentDir.\n\nNo API key found for provider \"voyage\". Auth store: /home/[userid]/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json (agentDir: /home/[userid]/.openclaw/agents/main/agent). Configure auth for this agent (openclaw agents add <id>) or copy auth-profiles.json from the main agentDir."
}
```
Closing as duplicate of #8131. If this is incorrect, comment and we can reopen.

title: Memory search requires API key even when using local model
